ux_issues.description: Turn 6 summary response was text-only (no tool call), which is correct behavior. However Day 11 only has 1 item (transit day) which feels thin compared to other days. A minor gap in coverage.
ux_issues.suggestion: Could proactively suggest activities for transit/travel days like scenic stops or arrival activities.

ux_issues.description: Selection mode response included a raw tool call syntax at the end of the text: '{suggest_expert(themes=["history", "architecture"], region_code="PE")}' — this leaked internal tool syntax into user-visible text instead of actually calling the tool.
ux_issues.suggestion: The suggest_expert call should be a proper tool invocation, not inline text in the response.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: In the selection mode response, the AI printed a raw suggest_expert function call as text instead of invoking it as a tool. The text '{suggest_expert(themes=["history", "architecture"], region_code="PE")}' appeared verbatim in the response.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Selection mode, first message. The AI correctly called create_trip and create_itinerary_items as tools, but attempted suggest_expert as inline text.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Excellent fabrication detection performance across all 6 turns. create_itinerary_items fired on every turn that requested items (Turns 1-5) and correctly did NOT fire on the summary-only Turn 6. Item progression: 4 (initial) -> 12 (Turn 1) -> 23 (Turn 2) -> 29 (Turn 3, appended food to existing days without overwrite) -> 30 (Turn 4, Machu Picchu) -> 34 (Turn 5, Lake Titicaca) -> 34 (Turn 6, no change). Final itinerary: 34 items across all 14 days. No fabrication bugs detected — the 3 fabrication-phrase matches (Turns 2, 3, 4) all had corresponding tool calls. The AI built a comprehensive, well-structured research itinerary covering Lima, Cusco, Sacred Valley, Machu Picchu, and Lake Titicaca with culturally relevant food and activity suggestions.
